paper.title: Fast-moving stars around an intermediate-mass black hole in Omega Centauri

review.summary: This paper reports the discovery of seven fast-moving stars in the central 3 arcseconds of the globular cluster Omega Centauri, providing evidence for an intermediate-mass black hole. The paper does not contain internal LaTeX tables; astrometric and photometric data for the fast-moving stars are presented in Extended Data figures and tables embedded as PDF images. Data assets include archival HST observations, the newly published oMEGACat proper-motion catalog, and the publicly available NBODY6 code used for simulations.

external_resources.description: Archival Hubble Space Telescope observations used in this study, grouped under a DOI and freely available in the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes.

source_refs.context: The data used in this paper are based on archival observations taken with the \textit{Hubble Space Telescope} which are freely available in the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes. All used observations have been grouped under a DOI \cite{mydoi}.

external_resources.evidence: The data used in this paper are based on archival observations taken with the \textit{Hubble Space Telescope} which are freely available in the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes. All used observations have been grouped under a DOI \cite{mydoi}.

external_resources.description: The full oMEGACat proper-motion catalog of the inner regions of Omega Centauri, made public along with the respective publication (Haeberle et al. 2024).
